texts.label: text
texts.orig: A type of porphyrin-chlorin heterodimers have been synthesized in a one-pot reaction, and their porphyrin and chlorin moieties are directly b , b 0 -linked through an sp 3 carbon. These species have been characterized by mass spectroscopy, 1 H NMR, X-ray crystallography, cyclic voltammetry, UV-vis and fluorescence spectroscopy.
texts.text: A type of porphyrin-chlorin heterodimers have been synthesized in a one-pot reaction, and their porphyrin and chlorin moieties are directly b , b 0 -linked through an sp 3 carbon. These species have been characterized by mass spectroscopy, 1 H NMR, X-ray crystallography, cyclic voltammetry, UV-vis and fluorescence spectroscopy.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Porphyrin dimers (including heterodimers) have been attracting considerable attention as models in light harvesting, as photosensitizers for photodynamic therapy (PDT), as molecular photonic and electronic wires, as catalysts, and as chirality probes in chiral recognition processes. 1 Directly linked porphyrin dimers are one interesting class of these compounds because of the interaction between the two porphyrin moieties. Since the type of bond connecting the porphyrin rings influences the electronic communication in the dimer, various synthetic strategies have been developed in order to make different dimers where the porphyrin units are directly linked at meso -meso , meso -b , and b -b positions. 2 Among them, b -b dimers are less common because of lengthy syntheses and low product yields. Only a limited number of approaches have been reported for the synthesis of b -b linked porphyrin dimers. 3 The development of new synthetic pathways for those species remains a considerable challenge. One general method to synthesize porphyrins, especially asymmetric porphyrins, is through a one-pot reaction by mixing different aldehydes and pyrrole. 4 Such method generally leads to multiple products. In some cases, unexpected products can be obtained, which can provide new methods for novel species. In our previous study, 5 we have reported the synthesis of an unexpected chiral porphyrin derivative which could be useful for different types of catalysis. 6 During a related study, we have found a general method to synthesize unprecedented b , b 0 -linked porphyrin-chlorin heterodimers in a one-pot reaction. Herein, we report the characterization of the resulting heterodimers by X-ray crystallography, mass spectroscopy, 1 H NMR, cyclic voltammetry, UV-vis and fluorescence spectroscopy, etc.
texts.text: Porphyrin dimers (including heterodimers) have been attracting considerable attention as models in light harvesting, as photosensitizers for photodynamic therapy (PDT), as molecular photonic and electronic wires, as catalysts, and as chirality probes in chiral recognition processes. 1 Directly linked porphyrin dimers are one interesting class of these compounds because of the interaction between the two porphyrin moieties. Since the type of bond connecting the porphyrin rings influences the electronic communication in the dimer, various synthetic strategies have been developed in order to make different dimers where the porphyrin units are directly linked at meso -meso , meso -b , and b -b positions. 2 Among them, b -b dimers are less common because of lengthy syntheses and low product yields. Only a limited number of approaches have been reported for the synthesis of b -b linked porphyrin dimers. 3 The development of new synthetic pathways for those species remains a considerable challenge. One general method to synthesize porphyrins, especially asymmetric porphyrins, is through a one-pot reaction by mixing different aldehydes and pyrrole. 4 Such method generally leads to multiple products. In some cases, unexpected products can be obtained, which can provide new methods for novel species. In our previous study, 5 we have reported the synthesis of an unexpected chiral porphyrin derivative which could be useful for different types of catalysis. 6 During a related study, we have found a general method to synthesize unprecedented b , b 0 -linked porphyrin-chlorin heterodimers in a one-pot reaction. Herein, we report the characterization of the resulting heterodimers by X-ray crystallography, mass spectroscopy, 1 H NMR, cyclic voltammetry, UV-vis and fluorescence spectroscopy, etc.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: both 1a and 1b are porphyrin dimers. 1 H NMR spectra of 1a and 1b display two resonances in the high field region, at � 1.22 and � 2.92 ppm for 1a , and � 1.20 and � 2.81 ppm for 1b . This indicates that there are two types of NH protons belonging to two different porphyrinoids. The one at higher field is similar to that of tetraphenylporphyrin, 8 and the one at lower field is similar to that of tetraphenylchlorin. 9 So 1 H NMR spectra and mass spectroscopy suggest that both 1a and 1b are possible porphyrin-chlorin heterodimers.
texts.text: both 1a and 1b are porphyrin dimers. 1 H NMR spectra of 1a and 1b display two resonances in the high field region, at � 1.22 and � 2.92 ppm for 1a , and � 1.20 and � 2.81 ppm for 1b . This indicates that there are two types of NH protons belonging to two different porphyrinoids. The one at higher field is similar to that of tetraphenylporphyrin, 8 and the one at lower field is similar to that of tetraphenylchlorin. 9 So 1 H NMR spectra and mass spectroscopy suggest that both 1a and 1b are possible porphyrin-chlorin heterodimers.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The above suggestion was confirmed by X-ray crystallography. X-Ray crystal structures of 1a and 2a have been determined. The detailed information is provided in ESI. w Structures show that 1a is a novel porphyrin-chlorin heterodimer and 2a is a porphyrin with an exocyclic ring. In the crystal structure of 1a , there are two independent heterodimer molecules and one of them is shown in Fig. 1. Such heterodimer is unique among porphyrinoids in that the dimeric linkage features a direct C b -C b bond between one porphyrin and one chlorin moiety. The porphyrin moiety is a tetraphenylporphyrin unit; while the chlorin moiety is a 2,3-disubstituted chlorin with an exocyclic ring. In the chlorin moiety, C(1b1) and C(1b2) are sp 3 hybridized carbons. C(1b1) is bonded to the porphyrin moiety and C(1b2) is bonded to C(120) to form a seven-membered ring. Because of the steric hinderance, it adopts a trans -configuration with two substituents on two different sides of the reduced pyrrole ring. The corresponding C-C distances range from 1.517 to 1.533 A , a typical distance for single bonds, which is consistent with the sp 3 hybridization feature of C(1b1) and C(1b2). Because of the asymmetric arrangement, both C(1b1) and C(1b2) are chiral carbons, so the single molecule is a chiral molecule. However, the whole crystal is achiral because it consists of pairs of enantiomers. The dihedral angle between the porphyrin and chlorin moieties is 71.85 1 and 77.31 1 for each heterodimer molecule. Center-tocenter distances in the two independent molecules are 8.978 and 8.893 A ˚ , respectively.
texts.text: The above suggestion was confirmed by X-ray crystallography. X-Ray crystal structures of 1a and 2a have been determined. The detailed information is provided in ESI. w Structures show that 1a is a novel porphyrin-chlorin heterodimer and 2a is a porphyrin with an exocyclic ring. In the crystal structure of 1a , there are two independent heterodimer molecules and one of them is shown in Fig. 1. Such heterodimer is unique among porphyrinoids in that the dimeric linkage features a direct C b -C b bond between one porphyrin and one chlorin moiety. The porphyrin moiety is a tetraphenylporphyrin unit; while the chlorin moiety is a 2,3-disubstituted chlorin with an exocyclic ring. In the chlorin moiety, C(1b1) and C(1b2) are sp 3 hybridized carbons. C(1b1) is bonded to the porphyrin moiety and C(1b2) is bonded to C(120) to form a seven-membered ring. Because of the steric hinderance, it adopts a trans -configuration with two substituents on two different sides of the reduced pyrrole ring. The corresponding C-C distances range from 1.517 to 1.533 A , a typical distance for single bonds, which is consistent with the sp 3 hybridization feature of C(1b1) and C(1b2). Because of the asymmetric arrangement, both C(1b1) and C(1b2) are chiral carbons, so the single molecule is a chiral molecule. However, the whole crystal is achiral because it consists of pairs of enantiomers. The dihedral angle between the porphyrin and chlorin moieties is 71.85 1 and 77.31 1 for each heterodimer molecule. Center-tocenter distances in the two independent molecules are 8.978 and 8.893 A ˚ , respectively.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the crystal packing, there are strong intermolecular interactions involved, especially edge to face interactions and p -p stacking interactions. In the unit cell, two heterodimer molecules orient differently. As shown in Fig. S5 (ESI w ), for one molecule, along the a axis, its porphyrin moiety interacts with the porphyrin moiety of another symmetry related molecule by p -p stacking. In addition, one phenyl ring of its chlorin moiety interacts with the neighbouring porphyrin moiety of another symmetry related molecule in an edge to face fashion with the carbon to porphyrin plane distance of 2.66 A . Its naphthyl group also interacts with C(2b5#2) in an edge to face fashion. For another heterodimer molecule, it packs in a similar pattern along the b axis as shown in Fig. S6 (ESI w ). The above intermolecular interactions stabilize the overall crystal structure.
texts.text: In the crystal packing, there are strong intermolecular interactions involved, especially edge to face interactions and p -p stacking interactions. In the unit cell, two heterodimer molecules orient differently. As shown in Fig. S5 (ESI w ), for one molecule, along the a axis, its porphyrin moiety interacts with the porphyrin moiety of another symmetry related molecule by p -p stacking. In addition, one phenyl ring of its chlorin moiety interacts with the neighbouring porphyrin moiety of another symmetry related molecule in an edge to face fashion with the carbon to porphyrin plane distance of 2.66 A . Its naphthyl group also interacts with C(2b5#2) in an edge to face fashion. For another heterodimer molecule, it packs in a similar pattern along the b axis as shown in Fig. S6 (ESI w ). The above intermolecular interactions stabilize the overall crystal structure.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Similar to the chlorin moiety in 1a , 2a has also a sevenmembered exocyclic ring as shown in Fig. 2. But it is a porphyrin species instead of chlorin. All the pyrrole carbons are sp 2 hybridized which is confirmed from their bond distances and angles. The dihedral angle between the naphthyl group and porphyrin core is 42.9 1 which is much smaller than that in 1a (51.60 1 and 53.4 1 ). It is also caused by the different hybridization of the b carbons.
texts.text: Similar to the chlorin moiety in 1a , 2a has also a sevenmembered exocyclic ring as shown in Fig. 2. But it is a porphyrin species instead of chlorin. All the pyrrole carbons are sp 2 hybridized which is confirmed from their bond distances and angles. The dihedral angle between the naphthyl group and porphyrin core is 42.9 1 which is much smaller than that in 1a (51.60 1 and 53.4 1 ). It is also caused by the different hybridization of the b carbons.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The structures suggest that the direct linkages between the porphyrin and chlorin moieties in the heterodimers are through sp 3 hybridized carbons. Such linkages also have some effect on the optical absorption data. Their UV-vis spectra are provided in Fig. S7 (ESI w ). Table 1 compares the electronic absorption data of 1a , 1b , 2a , 2b and the monomeric tetraphenylporphyrin (TPP), tetraphenylchlorin (TPC). For 2a and 2b with an exocyclic ring, their Soret (B) and Q band absorptions are clearly perturbed upon b -catenation of the monomer subunits. Compared to TPP, there is a bathochromic shift of the Soret bands (around 40 nm), and the Q bands shift to around 700 nm. Such shifts suggest electronic delocalization due to the exocyclic ring. For the heterodimers 1a and 1b , the spectra show two strong absorptions in the Soret band region
texts.text: The structures suggest that the direct linkages between the porphyrin and chlorin moieties in the heterodimers are through sp 3 hybridized carbons. Such linkages also have some effect on the optical absorption data. Their UV-vis spectra are provided in Fig. S7 (ESI w ). Table 1 compares the electronic absorption data of 1a , 1b , 2a , 2b and the monomeric tetraphenylporphyrin (TPP), tetraphenylchlorin (TPC). For 2a and 2b with an exocyclic ring, their Soret (B) and Q band absorptions are clearly perturbed upon b -catenation of the monomer subunits. Compared to TPP, there is a bathochromic shift of the Soret bands (around 40 nm), and the Q bands shift to around 700 nm. Such shifts suggest electronic delocalization due to the exocyclic ring. For the heterodimers 1a and 1b , the spectra show two strong absorptions in the Soret band region

texts.orig: Table 1 Comparison of UV-vis and fluorescence spectral parameters of 1a , 1b , 2a , 2b , tetraphenylporphyrin and tetraphenylchlorin
texts.text: Table 1 Comparison of UV-vis and fluorescence spectral parameters of 1a , 1b , 2a , 2b , tetraphenylporphyrin and tetraphenylchlorin

texts.label: text
texts.orig: and four weak absorptions in the Q band region. The overall spectra are a superposition of the respective spectra of the porphyrin and chlorin moieties. The one at around 420 nm is similar to that of TPP, another at around 435 nm can be assigned to the chlorin moiety with an exocyclic ring. The extinction coefficient of the Soret band of the chlorin moiety remains comparable to that in the unlinked macrocycle, while a significant reduction of the extinction coefficients of the porphyrin Soret band occurs. 12 For the Q band absorption at around 650 nm, it is much stronger for TPC than TPP. But for the heterodimers, its corresponding extinction coefficient is weaker than that of TPC. The reduction of extinction coefficients suggests weak coupling between porphyrin and chlorin moieties. Their fluorescence spectra have also been measured and provided in Fig. S8 (ESI w ). The preliminary results indicate that the peaks of heterodimers (as shown in Table 1) are located at similar positions as those in TPC. It seems that the fluorescence of the porphyrin moiety in the porphyrin-chlorin dimer disappeared. In a similar case of the ether-bonded porphyrin-chlorin system Zenkevich et al. explained that the fluorescence of the porphyrin moiety was quenched. 13 For these new species, the cyclic voltammograms have also been measured and shown in ESI. w For 1a and 1b , the spectra exhibit four oxidations, which correspond to two pairs of oxidation steps for both porphyrin and chlorin moieties, respectively. For 2a and 2b , there are two reversible oxidations followed by the third irreversible oxidation, which could be possibly related to the generation of fused porphyrins as in Scott et al. 's studies. 14
texts.text: and four weak absorptions in the Q band region. The overall spectra are a superposition of the respective spectra of the porphyrin and chlorin moieties. The one at around 420 nm is similar to that of TPP, another at around 435 nm can be assigned to the chlorin moiety with an exocyclic ring. The extinction coefficient of the Soret band of the chlorin moiety remains comparable to that in the unlinked macrocycle, while a significant reduction of the extinction coefficients of the porphyrin Soret band occurs. 12 For the Q band absorption at around 650 nm, it is much stronger for TPC than TPP. But for the heterodimers, its corresponding extinction coefficient is weaker than that of TPC. The reduction of extinction coefficients suggests weak coupling between porphyrin and chlorin moieties. Their fluorescence spectra have also been measured and provided in Fig. S8 (ESI w ). The preliminary results indicate that the peaks of heterodimers (as shown in Table 1) are located at similar positions as those in TPC. It seems that the fluorescence of the porphyrin moiety in the porphyrin-chlorin dimer disappeared. In a similar case of the ether-bonded porphyrin-chlorin system Zenkevich et al. explained that the fluorescence of the porphyrin moiety was quenched. 13 For these new species, the cyclic voltammograms have also been measured and shown in ESI. w For 1a and 1b , the spectra exhibit four oxidations, which correspond to two pairs of oxidation steps for both porphyrin and chlorin moieties, respectively. For 2a and 2b , there are two reversible oxidations followed by the third irreversible oxidation, which could be possibly related to the generation of fused porphyrins as in Scott et al. 's studies. 14

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In conclusion, our current work provides a general approach to novel porphyrin-chlorin species in which porphyrin and chlorin moieties are b , b 0 -linked through sp 3 chlorin carbons. The reaction mechanism is still under investigation. One speculation is that during the reaction, the phlorin may play an important role as an intermediate. The fact that we cannot obtain phlorin species is consistent with the instability of the phlorin as reported in the literature. 15 We also preformed experiments with different ethanol content. When the ethanol content was as low as 1%, we obtained the mixture of 1a , 2a and 3a in a yield of 3%, 6% and 5%, respectively. When the ethanol content was higher, more 3a and less 1a / 2a were obtained. Such results suggest that there are competitive reactions which lead to different products. Considering the structural relation between phlorin and compounds 1 , 2 , 3 , phlorin could be converted to compound 3 in the presence of ethanol or be converted into compound 1 or 2 in the absence of ethanol. Further studies on their metallic complexes, the energy transfer processes and the development of such heterodimer systems are underway.
texts.text: In conclusion, our current work provides a general approach to novel porphyrin-chlorin species in which porphyrin and chlorin moieties are b , b 0 -linked through sp 3 chlorin carbons. The reaction mechanism is still under investigation. One speculation is that during the reaction, the phlorin may play an important role as an intermediate. The fact that we cannot obtain phlorin species is consistent with the instability of the phlorin as reported in the literature. 15 We also preformed experiments with different ethanol content. When the ethanol content was as low as 1%, we obtained the mixture of 1a , 2a and 3a in a yield of 3%, 6% and 5%, respectively. When the ethanol content was higher, more 3a and less 1a / 2a were obtained. Such results suggest that there are competitive reactions which lead to different products. Considering the structural relation between phlorin and compounds 1 , 2 , 3 , phlorin could be converted to compound 3 in the presence of ethanol or be converted into compound 1 or 2 in the absence of ethanol. Further studies on their metallic complexes, the energy transfer processes and the development of such heterodimer systems are underway.
